---
title: "Plot composition"
format: html
---

ggnomics uses **plotnine's native composition system** (plotnine ≥ 0.15).
Any ggnomics plot function returns a `ggplot` object that can be combined
with standard operators — no extra dependencies needed.

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

import importlib.util
_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("mock_data", "00_mock_data.py")
_m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(_spec); _spec.loader.exec_module(_m)

adata = _m.make_mock_anndata(n_cells=500, n_genes=50, seed=42)
de_df = _m.make_mock_de_results(n_genes=500, seed=42)
```

## Basic operators

### Side by side ( `|` )

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_umap, plot_violin_stats

p_umap  = plot_umap(adata, color="cluster", title="UMAP")
p_violin = plot_violin_stats(adata, "Gene0001", group_by="cluster",
                              sig_only=False, title="Gene0001")

p_umap | p_violin
```

### Stacked ( `/` )

```{python}
p_umap / p_violin
```

### With spacer

```{python}
from plotnine.composition import plot_spacer

p_umap | plot_spacer() | p_violin
```

## Apply theme to all panels ( `&` )

```{python}
from plotnine import theme_minimal

(p_umap | p_violin) & theme_minimal()
```

## Complex layout

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_dot, plot_volcano

p_dot   = plot_dot(adata, features=["Gene0001","Gene0002","Gene0003"],
                   group_by="cluster", title="Dot plot")
p_vol   = plot_volcano(de_df, gene_col="gene", label_top_n=3, title="Volcano")

(p_umap | p_vol) / p_dot
```

## `grid` helper — equal-sized grid

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_embedding_panel

comp = plot_embedding_panel(
    adata,
    features=[f"Gene{i+1:04d}" for i in range(6)],
    ncol=3,
    shared_scale=True,
)
comp
```

## Composition-level annotation (plotnine ≥ 0.16)

```{python}
from ggnomics import annotate_composition

comp_ann = annotate_composition(
    p_umap | p_violin,
    title="UMAP and expression",
    subtitle="500 mock cells · 5 clusters",
    caption="Generated with ggnomics",
)
comp_ann
```

## Saving a composition

```{python}
# Same API as ggplot.save()
(p_umap | p_violin).save("/tmp/my_figure.png", dpi=150)
print("Saved to /tmp/my_figure.png")
```

## `ggnomics` composition helpers

The `hstack`, `vstack`, `grid`, and `annotate_composition` helpers are
exported from `ggnomics` directly:

```{python}
from ggnomics import hstack, vstack, grid, annotate_composition

# hstack with spacers
hstack(p_umap, p_violin, spacer=True)
```

```{python}
# vstack
vstack(p_umap, p_violin)
```

```{python}
# grid: uses plot_layout(ncol=...) on plotnine ≥ 0.16
grid([p_umap, p_violin, p_dot], ncol=2)
```

## Width / height control (plotnine ≥ 0.16)

```{python}
from plotnine.composition import plot_layout, plot_annotation

# 2:1 width ratio
(p_umap | p_violin) + plot_layout(widths=[2, 1])
```

```{python}
# Apply whole-figure title
(p_umap | p_violin | p_dot) + plot_annotation(
    title="Three panels",
    subtitle="plotnine 0.16 native composition",
)
```
